title: Use 2 spaces for indentation
description: All CSS indentation must use exactly 2 spaces. Declarations should be indented one level from their selector.

title: Use Unix line endings (LF)
description: CSS files must use Unix line endings (LF) rather than Windows (CRLF) or Mac (CR) line endings.

title: End files with single blank line
description: CSS files should end with exactly one blank line, no more, no less.

title: Remove trailing whitespace
description: Lines should not have any whitespace characters at the end.

title: Use one selector per line for multiple selectors
description: When using multiple selectors, place each selector on its own line for better readability.

title: Quote attribute values in selectors
description: Always use quotes around attribute values in CSS selectors.

title: End declarations with semicolons
description: Every CSS declaration must end with a semicolon, including the last declaration in a rule.

title: Use double quotes for quoted values
description: When quotes are needed in CSS values, use double quotes rather than single quotes.

title: Prefer rem units over px
description: Use rem units for sizing to improve accessibility and responsive design.

title: Omit units for zero values
description: Do not include units when the value is zero (0px should be 0).

title: Order properties logically
description: Order CSS properties in a logical sequence: positioning, box model, then other declarations.

title: Use component-based CSS architecture
description: Structure CSS using SMACSS methodology with Base, Layout, Component, State, and Theme categories.

title: Use BEM-like naming for CSS classes
description: Use clear naming conventions: .component-name, .component-name--variant, .component-name__sub-object.

title: Use .is- prefix for state classes
description: State classes that represent temporary appearance changes should use the .is- prefix.

title: Use .js- prefix for JavaScript hooks
description: Classes used solely for JavaScript targeting should use the .js- prefix and have no CSS styling.

title: Use functional pseudo-classes for combining selectors
description: Use functional pseudo-classes like :is(), :not(), or :where() to combine selectors when possible.

title: Use space after commas in property values
description: Include a space after commas in property values for better readability.

title: Add LTR comments for directional properties
description: For properties without logical property equivalents, add /* LTR */ comment and create additional RTL ruleset.

title: Place vendor prefixes before non-prefixed version
description: Place vendor-prefixed properties directly before their non-prefixed version.

title: Use Doxygen-style multi-line comments
description: Use Doxygen-style comments for multi-line descriptions and file headers.

title: Use ES6+ syntax
description: Use modern JavaScript syntax including arrow functions template literals and destructuring.

title: Use Drupal.behaviors pattern
description: All JavaScript that interacts with the DOM must use the Drupal.behaviors pattern for AJAX compatibility.

title: Use once() for event handlers
description: Use the once() function from Drupal core to prevent duplicate event handlers on AJAX updates.

title: Use Drupal.t() for translatable strings
description: All user-facing strings must use Drupal.t() for translation support.

title: Use strict mode
description: Always enable strict mode within IIFEs using 'use strict'.

title: Use Drupal attributes object properly
description: Always use the attributes object for element attributes instead of hardcoding.

title: Use BEM class naming in templates
description: Apply BEM naming conventions consistently in Twig templates using clean_class filter.

title: Proper escaping in templates
description: Understand and correctly apply Twig auto-escaping. Use |raw only when content is trusted.
